parts.answer: The only correct answer is C (potassium bromide) A is not correct because the least exothermic lattice energy is between the largest ions with the smallest charge and calcium ions are smaller and have a higher charge than potassium ions B is not correct because the least exot hermic lattice energy is between the la rgest ions with the smallest charge and magnesium ions are smaller and have a higher charge than potassium ions D is not correct because sodium i ons are smaller than potassium ions (1) Question Number Mark

The only correct answer is B (magnesium chloride ) A is not correct because Ca 2+ ions are larger than magnesi um ions so will polarise less C is not correct because K + ions are larger than magnesium ions and ha ve a lower charge so will polarise less D is not correct because Na + ions are larger than magnesium ions and have a lower charge so will polarise less (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is C (the total entropy when KC l dissolves is positive) A is not correct because the enthalpy change of hydrati on for all ions and lattice en ergy for all ionic compounds are exothermic so this does not explain why KCl is soluble B is not correct because the enthalpy change of hydrati on for all ions and lattice en ergy for all ionic compounds are exothermic D is not correct because the total entropy mu st be positive for a spontaneous reaction (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is A (3.61 x 10 − ) B is not correct because R and ∆ S total are the wrong way up C is not correct because the temperature should not be included D is not correct because the negative sign for ∆ S total has been omitted (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is A (CH COCH + H + ⇌ CH C(O + H)CH fast, CH C(O + H)CH → CH C(OH)=CH + H + slow, CH C(OH)=CH + I → CH COCH I + HI fast) B is not correct because the steps up to and including the slow step must include CH COCH and H + ions and I must only be involved in a fast step C is not correct because the steps up to and including the slow step must include CH COCH and H + ions and I must only be involved in a fast step D is not correct because the steps up to and including the slow step must include CH COCH and H + ions and I must only be involved in a fast step (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is C (1.0 x 10 − ) A is not correct because the initial rate and the rate constant have been mixed up B is not correct because [A] has not been squared D is not correct because [B] parts.answer: The only correct answer is B (1.95) A is not correct because pK a has not been converted to K a C is not correct because this is the pH of 0.100 mol dm − ethanoic acid D is not correct because the square root of K a x [CH ClCOOH] has not been used to calculate [H + ] (1) Question Number Mark

The only correct answer is C (Acid: CH ClCOOH, Conjugate base: CH ClCOO − ) A is not correct because ethanoic acid has a higher pK a than chloroethanoic acid so acts as a base in this reaction B is not correct because ethanoic acid has a higher pK a than chloroethanoic acid so acts as a base in this reaction D is not correct because chloroethanoic ac id loses a proton when it acts as an acid (1) Question Number Mark

The only correct answer is B (region U ) A is not correct because in region T there is only aqueous ammonia at the start of the titration C is not correct because in region V , the vertical part of the graph, represents the end- point of the titration D is not correct because in region W all the aqueous ammonia has been neutralised (1) Question Number Mark

The only correct answer is A (methyl red) B is not correct because phenol red has a pH range of 6.8 to 8.4 and 8.4 is not in the vertical region C is not correct because phenolphthalein has a pH range of 8.2 to 10.0 and this is not in the vertical region D is not correct because thymol blue has a pH range of 1.2 to 2.8 and this is not in the vertical region (1) Question Number Mark

The only correct answer is B (5.8) A is not correct because this is the approximat e pH when excess hydrochloric acid has been added C is not correct because this is the approximate pH ne ar the start of the end point when there is still excess aqueous ammonia D is not correct because this is th e approximate pH of aqueous ammonia (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is B (9.77 x 10 − (mol dm − )) A is not correct because the two volumes have been reversed C is not correct because a mole ratio of 1:1 has b een used instead of 1 mol of acid : 2 mol NaOH D is not correct because a mole ratio of 2 mol of acid : 1 mol NaOH has been used (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is C (tertiary only) A is not correct because primary bromoalkanes react by an S N 2 mechanism and the product would be optically active B is not correct because secondary bromoalkanes react by an S N 1 or an S N 2 mechanism and the product could be optically active D is not correct because primary an d secondary bromoalkanes react by S N 1 and S N 2 mechanisms and the p roducts could be opticall y active (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is D A is not correct because this is an aldehyde and it would give a silver mirror with Tollens’ reagent B is not correct because this is an aldehyde and it would give a silver mirror with Tollens’ reagent C is not correct because this is an aldeh y de and it would g ive a silver mirror with Tollens’ rea g ent (1) Question Number Mark The only correct answer is C ( CH COONa and CH CH CH OH) A is not correct because ethanoic acid reac ts with NaOH to form the sodium salt B is not correct because ethanoic acid reacts with Na OH to form the sodium salt and propan-1-ol does not react with NaOH D is not correct because Units in any order e.g. J mol − K − Penalise incorrect / incomplete units in ∆ S total but allow: e.g. J / mol/ K or J/mol.K mol − and K − in otherwise correct units Correct answer with no working scores (5) (5) Number Additional Guidance Mark  working for at least one half-life shown on graph  values of two half-lives  first order and because the half-lives ar e constant / the same / similar (1) (1) (1) Example of half-lives: half-lives may be written or clearly shown on graph first half-life = 200 s and second half-life = 200 s ± 20 s Allow 2 nd half-life = 400 – 200 = 200 s Stand alone mark Do not award zero order / second order (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  first order with respect to BrO − ions and because in runs 1 and 2 as [BrO − ] doubles (and [H + ] is constant) the rate doubles  in runs 1 and 3, as [BrO − ] triples rate should triple (to 1.08 x 10 − ) and [H + ] also doubles and rate increases by a factor of 4  so reaction is second order with respect to H + ions (1) (1) (1) In M1 and M2, the working may be shown in the table Allow implied runs e.g. as [BrO − ] doubles (and [H + ] is constant), the rate doubles Allow correct a
